therapy, but rather on the beneficiary’s need for skilled care. Assuming all other 
eligibility and coverage requirements have been met, skilled speech-language pathology 
services are covered when the individualized assessment of the patient’s clinical 
condition demonstrates that the specialized judgment, knowledge, and skills of a 
qualified speech-language pathologist are necessary.
